rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Special Motion To Strike Petitioner'S Amended Civil Petition Pursuant To Ccp 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2024 line 1. RESPONDENT RAY YEGANEH NOTICE OF SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PETITIONER'S AMENDED CIVIL PETITION PURSUANT TO CCP 425.16 is DENIED. The Court notes that the First Amended Petition is largely incomprehensible. Notwithstanding the uncertainty of the Petition, Defendant filed this motion seeking to strike the petition and 4 causes of action therein. Defendant does not identify the purported causes of action. Defendant further does not identify any claims upon which relief may be granted, that defendant believes are subject to ANTI-SLAPP. For example, Defendant does not address how the claim for premises liability or product liability arises out of protected activity and what that protected activity may be. Defendant's notice of Motion points to allegations on page 5 and 6 of the First Amended Petition. Those allegations appear to state that a lien for a certain amount was recoded on a date certain and that an abstract of judgment for a certain amount was recorded on a date certain. Defendant cites to no authority and provides no argument to support their assertion that the recordation of this lien or recordation of this abstract of judgement is protected activity within the meaning on CCP 425.16(e)(1)-(4). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TROY SNOWDEN VS. S&S HOSPITALITY INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2024 line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT TROY SNOWDEN, SUMMER SALIBA MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is DENIED. Moving party fails to carry its burden and show that any part of the Cross-Complaint is false, irrelevant or improper. The Court will not find that the Cross-Complaint was untimely filed as the Court records indicate that Cross-Complainant attempted to file the Cross-Complaint on June 25,2024. The Court notes that the delay could have been avoided had cross-complainant presented an order on the motion for leave to file a cross-complaint at the hearing. The order would have been executed on June 25, 2024 at the hearing and any delay would have been avoided. The Court further notes that Cross-Complainant failed to properly spell their name on the Cross-Complaint to match it with the other filings in this case "Steve" vs. "Steven." The appropriate spelling of the name would avoid any further delay. Declaration of Ms. Chau in Support of Motion to Strike is stricken except for paragraph 21. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2024 line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT TROY SNOWDEN, SUMMER SALIBA NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR CODE CIV. rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE CHU VS. RAY YEGANEH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Special Motion To Strike Comlpaint Pursuant To Ccp 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2024 line 7. DEFENDANT RAY YEGANEH NOTICE OF SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMLPAINT PURSUANT TO CCP 425.16 is DENIED. The Court notes that the First Amended Petition is largely incomprehensible. Notwithstanding the uncertainty of the Petition, Defendant filed this motion seeking to strike the petition and 4 causes of action therein. Defendant does not identify the purported causes of action. Defendant further does not identify any claims upon which relief may be granted, that defendant believes are subject to ANTI-SLAPP. For example, Defendant does not address how the claim for breach of contract arises out of protected activity and what that protected activity may be in relation to the claim for breach of contract. Defendant's notice of Motion points to allegations on page 4 and 5 of the Complaint. Those allegations appear to state that a lien for a certain amount was recoded. Defendant cites to no authority and provides no argument to support their assertion that the recordation of this lien is protected activity within the meaning on CCP 425.16(e)(1)-(4). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE T. LUI ET AL VS. GARY LUI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment For Possession Of Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2024 line 15. PLAINTIFF LAWRENCE LUI , AS CO-TRUSTEES OF THE LUI FAMILY TRUST DATED AUGUST 27, 2005, TERRY LUI, GLORIA LEE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / NOTICE OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FOR POSSESSION OF REAL PROPERTY is DENIED. Moving party failed to cite any authority to support Plaintiff's assertion that defendants were unlawfully detaining the property at the time of the filing of the complaint, which served as a notice of termination of license, i.e. before Defendants were notified of the termination of license via Complaint. Motion for Summary Judgment is framed by the pleadings. Additionally, Plaintiffs fail to provide admissible competent evidence that defendants remain in possession or that defendants paid no consideration for occupancy of the premises. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
